FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Strong Q2 bank earnings expected amid geopolitical volatility, but rate-hike odds rising could pressure NIMs; meanwhile, regulatory shifts under Trump and fintech competition heating up.

bullets.text: Big banks poised for Q2 revenue boost from SpaceX IPO advisory fees, Iran war trading volatility, and pickup in commercial lending—strong earnings backdrop.
Fed rate-hike odds for July rising on geopolitical oil-price spike—direct headwind to NIM compression and deposit-pricing dynamics for banks.
Trump's CFPB nominee Brian Johnson wins 60-day post-confirmation review on staff cuts, giving Trump pick discretion over bureau workforce reduction.
Kalshi launches 'Pro' product for multi-market perpetual futures trading—CFTC-regulated fintech expanding institutional capabilities in prediction markets.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: Russell Investments' $580B CalPERS acquisition dominates; RIA consolidation accelerates with leadership moves and platform restructuring across the sector.

bullets.text: CalPERS acquiring Russell Investments in transformative deal—Russell sheds debt burden and converts to non-profit governance, positioning itself as next Vanguard-like reputational powerhouse.
RIA consolidation hitting new speeds: Hightower organic growth surges, ex-Fido CTO raises $7.7M for standalone platform, Choreo closes fourth deal, and Wealthfront crosses $100B AUM.
Aspen-backed Summitry ($4B RIA) undergoes succession—founder Higgins steps to chair, elevating President Katz and COO Wilkes to co-CEOs after 22-year tenure.
Betterment sued over cash sweep practices; class action alleges firm steered client cash into non-interest-bearing accounts at partner banks, capturing hidden spreads without client benefit.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: KKR joins A$7.7B Steadfast auction while US P&C underwriting hits decade highs, though AI cyber risks and product liability headwinds create cross-currents.

bullets.text: KKR + Amwins + Dragoneer consortium bids A$7.7B ($5.33B) for Australian broker Steadfast, signaling continued PE appetite for insurance distribution.
US P&C industry posts strongest underwriting profit in a decade with $84B gains over two years and lowest combined ratio in 10 years, but performance diverges sharply by line.
Canada's banking regulator warns institutions on cyber risks from advanced AI models like Anthropic's Claude, citing compressed response windows and expanded threat surfaces.
FDA tightens infant-formula supplier oversight after botulism recalls—first US outbreak in decades, creating new liability exposure for manufacturers.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Mastercard mulls Vocalink exit while CMBS distress signals trouble ahead; meanwhile, fintech expansion hits friction.

bullets.text: Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink, the UK payments platform acquired from British banks in 2016—potential return to original owners.
CMBS special servicing rate climbed in June 2026 as retail, office, and lodging loans deteriorate; expect servicer workouts ahead.
North America remains LP-favored in private debt despite headwinds; performing/distressed credit divergence widening.
Klarna faces Dutch class action lawsuit as fintech draws regulatory scrutiny in key European market.

PE & Deals.lede: Arctos pioneers a novel GP financing structure while the secondaries market heats up on multiple fronts—from Moelis' LP-led expansion to strong seller activity.

bullets.text: Arctos Partners closed a $6.2B GP financing deal using an innovative 'self-liquidating' Keystone structure that lets the fund return capital to LPs while keeping portfolio exposure—a first-time deployment in GP financing.
Moelis is building out LP-led secondaries with a four-person team poached from Evercore, signaling aggressive expansion into a growth segment of the secondary markets.
LP-led secondaries market is booming as major players (LGT, Dawson, Pomona) actively source deals, driven by favorable pricing and liquidity conditions attracting sellers.
Korean pension KBIZ plans to deploy ~$230M into domestic blind-pool PE/VC funds in 2026, signaling institutional capital rotation toward Korea-focused managers.

Markets & Macro.lede: Bank earnings season opens amid QE structural concerns; small caps rally while UK gilt yields spike past 5% on energy inflation.

bullets.text: Major bank earnings kick off Tuesday with high expectations and risk of disappointment—a pivotal week for setting tone on sector health.
QE fundamentally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making QT riskier than policymakers acknowledge because banks now depend on central bank liquidity support.
Russell 2000 tracking best year since 2003 as investors rotate from mega-cap tech into small caps on AI capex shift and tax policy tailwinds.
UK 10-year gilt yields exceed 5% on oil surge just as new PM takes office—market signaling fiscal sustainability concerns amid energy pass-through inflation.

Newsletters.lede: Big bank earnings dominate, with ETF outflows, portfolio tax friction solutions, and structural shifts in student lending reshaping advisory workflows.

bullets.text: Five of six largest US banks report Q2 earnings tomorrow—credit quality, deposit dynamics, and consumer health will be under the microscope across lending, trading, and wealth management.
ETF flows went negative in a rare week; redemptions hit equity, bond, and alternative products as market volatility or rotation drives investor behavior.
Vanguard and Envestnet partnership tackles a real RIA pain point—tax friction when converting client portfolios between model allocations.
Student loan policy shifts are forcing advisors to rebuild college planning playbooks, reshaping demand for education financing products.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

Learn.lede: 5 stories spanning incentive misalignment, AI moat shifts, market theory, and Apple's AI strategy gap.

bullets.text: Open-source AI models now match frontier capabilities at 1/10th cost, forcing founders to compete on moats beyond raw performance rather than model superiority.
Apple's OpenAI lawsuit over alleged employee trade-secret theft is a smokescreen—the real issue is Apple lacks coherent AI strategy and is falling behind competitors.
NBA study models how individual performance metrics (shooting %) incentivize players to take low-value end-of-quarter heaves, creating classic principal-agent misalignment.
Tyler Cowen argues P ≠ NP is necessary for competitive markets: if P = NP, firms could efficiently detect and sustain cartels; computational intractability preserves competition.

all.lede: Major M&A activity in insurance and payments, US bank earnings season begins, and structural QT risks emerge.

bullets.text: KKR joins Amwins and Dragoneer to bid A$7.7B ($5.33B) for Australian broker Steadfast, intensifying competition in global insurance consolidation.
Mastercard exploring sale of Vocalink (UK payments platform acquired 2016), potentially divesting from the UK payments infrastructure game.
Six largest US banks report Q2 earnings Tuesday; results will expose credit quality, deposit trends, and consumer health across major business lines.
QE structurally rewired bank balance sheets and funding models, making quantitative tightening riskier than policymakers recognize due to banks' dependence on central bank liquidity.
